items.headline: Can ASTS Thrive Despite Rising Competition in the SATCOM Space?
items.summary: AST Spacemobile expands telecom partnerships and targets 45 BlueBird satellites in 2026, but rising competition and execution risks could test its path.
items.why_it_matters: News may increase risk, valuation pressure, or negative sentiment.

items.headline: How America Got to SpaceX: The Long Trail of Big IPOs That Began in 1783
items.summary: SpaceX’s  $75 billion initial public offering shattered market records, turned founder Elon Musk into the world’s first trillionaire, and opened fresh debates over corporate valuations and governance.  It’s not the first time an IPO has shaken things up.  IPOs have been reshaping markets since the Bank of North America’s 1783 debut got it all started for the young republic.
items.why_it_matters: News appears more supportive of sentiment or growth expectations.

items.headline: Verizon launches simpler plans and new loyalty program, drops some fees
items.summary: Verizon launches new Simplicity plans dropping activation and upgrade fees while introducing a loyalty program with 3% back on bills and brand perks.
items.why_it_matters: News appears more supportive of sentiment or growth expectations.

items.headline: AT&T names Jennifer Biry as CFO from 2027, Desroches to retire
items.summary: Telecom provider AT&T said on Tuesday Pascal ‌Desroches will retire as CFO ‌at the end of 2026 and that ​Jennifer Biry would succeed him. Here are more details: • Jennifer Biry, the former CFO of McAfee, is set ‌to become ⁠AT&T's CFO at the start of 2027, according to ⁠a company filing. Biry was appointed AT&T's deputy CFO on Monday.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: Cisco Systems Inc (CSCO) Partners With AT&T. It’s About Cars
items.summary: Cisco Systems Inc (NASDAQ:CSCO) is one of billionaire Ken Fisher’s top high growth stock picks. In the past month, Cisco shares have soared more than 20%. The stock is up more than 58% year-to-date. Cisco Systems Inc (NASDAQ:CSCO) has landed a role in AT&T’s in-car entertainment platform.
